titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Division of Nutritional Sciences records29-1-3247
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Eileen Keating

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-03-10 23:12:31 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Division of Nutritional Sciences records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Division of Nutritional Sciences

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Human Nutrition and Food

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Food and Nutrition

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Printed Materials, Publications, Reports, Photographs, Financial Records, CDs
did.abstract: Includes correspondence, reports, grants, course syllabi, meeting minutes, program files, awards, photographs, and department reviews concerning the Division.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: RMC Staff, January 2007 Daniel W. Young, February 2010

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, faculty annual reports (some on CDs), grants, course syllabi (on CDs), meeting minutes, program files, awards, photographs, department reviews concerning the Division, and material concerning the Cornell Food and Nutrition Policy Program (CFNPP).

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: See also collections #21-24-3292, Expanded Food and Nutrition Education Program Records; #21-24-1975, Cornell University Cooperative Extension Records; and #23-2-2817, Cornell University College of Human Ecology Records; #29-4-2733, Division of Nutritional Sciences Cooperative Extension Nutrition Program Records for related material.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Committees Box 10- Folder: 1-16; Box 18- Folder: 7
Series II. Correspondence Box 8- Folder: 8-34; Box 9- Folders: 1-32
Series III. Directories and Rosters Box 1- Folder: 2
Series IV. Events and Visitors Box 1- Folder: 15,20; Box 9- Folders: 33-34; Box 14- Folder :31
Series V. Facilities Box 1- Folder: 15, 26-38; Box 11- Folders: 1
Series VI. Faculty and Course Syllabi Box 7- Folder: 1-23; Box 13- Folders: 9
Series VIII. Grants and Proposals Box 5- Folder: 13-31; Box 6- Folders: 1-24
Series IX. Programs Box 1; Box 3- Folder: 1-36; Box 4- Folders: 1-33; Box 5- Folders: 1-12; Box 11- Folders: 2-5; Box 18- Folder: 8; Box 22- Folder: 12
Series X. Publications Box 1- Folder: 14, 16-17, 22; Box 13- Folders: 1-11; Box 18- Folders: 1-4
Series XI. Reports; Faculty Annual Reports Box 1- Folder: 21; Box 10- Folders: 17-28; Box 14- Folders: 1-54; Box 15- Folders: 1-28; Box 18- Folder: 9; Box 22
Series XII. Reviews and Strategic Plans Box 1- Folder: 1, 4-13, 24; Box 7- Folders: 24-32; Box 8- Folders: 1-7
Series XIII. Scholarships/Awards Box 2- Folder: 1-19; Box 18- Folder: 10-26
Series XIV. University Concerns Box 10- Folder: 29-35
Series XV. Photographs and Slides Box 12- Folder: 1-31; Box 18- Folder: 5-6

odd.head: General
odd.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/11733

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Division of Nutritional Sciences records, #29-1-3247. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: On March 14, 1974 the Board of Trustees voted to abolish the Graduate School of Nutrition and the Department of Human Nutrition and Food in the College of Human Ecology and to establish the Division of Nutritional Sciences, an intercollege unit within the Colleges of Agriculture and Life Sciences and Human Ecology.

Crawl of Cornell NutritionWorks website by Archive-It

dao.xlink:title: Crawl of Cornell NutritionWorks website by Archive-It [Delivery]

daodesc.p: Crawl of Cornell NutritionWorks website by Archive-It [Delivery]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This website was created by the Division of Nutritional Sciences to provide online professional development. The Course Catalog listed written and video presentations by experts on a variety of food and nutrition topics. Members could earn personalized certificates of completion for continuing education credit on some topics. Brief articles on current food, nutrition, and food safety topics were also available under Timely Topics. Over its existence, a total of 26,000 people from 190 countries registered as members.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: This website was created to replace and expand upon the original Division of Nutritional Sciences Food and Nutrition website. It was funded primarily through grants from USDA.

did.unittitle: Video presentations from the Cornell NutritionWorks website

dao.xlink:title: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

daodesc.p: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

did.unittitle: Preventing Childhood Obesity: Online Training Course

dao.xlink:title: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

daodesc.p: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This online course was designed to train Cooperative Extension and other community nutrition and youth development educators nationally in a new approach to preventing childhood obesity in their communities. The focus was on an ecological approach that involved assessing behavioral, environmental and other factors that contributed to obesity and then determining interventions to change those factors in order to support healthy food and activity behaviors in the community. By the end of the course, participants had created a four-part action plan describing a collaborative approach to preventing childhood obesity customized for their community.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The course was offered as a facilitated 6 week course and participants were encouraged to enroll as teams from the same community. In 20 sessions, over 750 people from 50 states and territories and 23 countries enrolled. Of these, 635 people completed the course.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The course was developed and delivered in partnership with eCornell. The course development was funded by a Faculty Innovation in Teaching grant from Cornell and the delivery and evaluation was funded primarily by a grant from USDA's Agriculture and Food Research Initiative (AFRI).

dao.xlink:title: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

daodesc.p: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The website was created to provide up-to-date food and nutrition information online. It included an "Ask the Nutrition Expert" feature which provided a written overview on a topic by a Division of Nutritional Sciences expert and offered the opportunity for people to ask the expert a question. Brief articles on current food, nutrition, and food safety topics were also available under Timely Topics.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Funding for this website was provided primarily through USDA Smith-Lever funds.

did.unittitle: Programming for Infant and Young Child Feeding: Online Training Course

dao.xlink:title: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

daodesc.p: Preventing childhood obesity, Food and nutrition website videos, Cornell nutrition website (98-2002), UNICEF website course

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This online course was developed to enhance the competencies and build the capacity of UNICEF staff and their counterparts worldwide who are involved in Infant and Young Child Feeding (IYCF) programs in low-and middle-income countries. The content covered IYCF program development, implementation and evaluation. The course was developed in collaboration with UNICEF and was offered for free. More than 14,500 Cornell NutritionWorks members from 181 countries registered for the UNICEF course and more than 4,700 people from 150 countries completed the course.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The course development, delivery, and evaluation were funded by a grant from UNICEF.

did.unittitle: Crawl of UNICEF site by Archive-It

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An archived version of the UNICEF course: Programming for Infant and Young Child Feeding website can be found in Archive-It: https://wayback.archive-it.org/2566/20190812123506/https://www.nutritionworks.cornell.edu/UNICEF/home.cfm. The promotional page can be found here:Cornell UNICEF course archived website
